spec_match.rationale: Connector uses anchor-size() inside `left`, `width`, AND `top` simultaneously — exactly the spec change. Label similarly uses anchor-size() in inset properties.

interactive.rationale: Slider drives a CSS-only rescale of both connector and label; support probe creates a sniffer div and tests the parser.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises 'drag the slider and watch the line and label rescale' — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Support probe only checks `left: anchor-size(width)`; cannot distinguish a browser that supports anchor-size() in width (pre-131) from one that supports it in inset (131+) — feature detection is overly optimistic and may falsely report 'supported'.

references.rationale: See also links to chromestatus.com/feature/5189336679743488 — WRONG feature ID. This concept is part of feature 5203950077476864. The mistake breaks rule #3's self-heal: if the folder is renamed, fix-slugs.py will identify it as a different feature.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables used throughout, accent-blue used consistently for both line and label, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Fix the wrong chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: See also currently points at chromestatus.com/feature/5189336679743488 but this concept belongs to feature 5203950077476864 (the 'allow anchor-size() in inset and margin' feature). Correct the URL — without this, fix-slugs.py would relocate the folder under a different feature.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the feature-detection probe
openQuestions.detail: `probe.style.left = 'anchor-size(width)'` returns truthy on any browser that supports anchor-size() at all, including pre-131 where it's only valid in sizing. Add a second probe: `probe.style.marginLeft = 'anchor-size(width)'`. If margin-left accepts the value, we are on 131+.

summary: Demonstrates the use-case beautifully but ships the wrong chromestatus feature ID in see-also, a real CLAUDE.md rule #3 regression. Feature-detection probe also reads too optimistically.
